This repository contains the source documents for the [cltl.github.io](https://cltl.github.io) pages.

## Repository Setup

The website is written with [Docusaurus](https://docusaurus.io/en/) instead of Jekyll. The setup on GitHub involves two distinct branches, `master` and `source-docs`:

* `master`: this branch contains the html pages. These pages are generated by Docusaurus, so you should not need to commit anything here through `git`, this README excepted.
* `source-docs`: this branch contains the content of these site, and is where you will pull/push your commits. 

### README and ME and ME

This repository contains three README files:

* on `master`: this README contains information on the GitHub setup of this website, and instructions to commit changes and publish them.
* on `source-docs`: the [top-level README](https://github.com/cltl/cltl.github.io/blob/source-docs/README.md) provides brief instructions to modify the content. The [README located in `website`](https://github.com/cltl/cltl.github.io/blob/source-docs/website/README.md) provides additional information on content modification and pointers to the Docusaurus documentation.

## Contributing to this website

Contributing to this website requires two steps:

- [Modifying the content](#modifying-the-content). You only need `git` for this in principle.
- [Publishing the site](#publishing-the-site). You will need `Node >= 8.x` and `[Yarn](https://classic.yarnpkg.com/en/docs/install#mac-stable) >= 1.5` for this. `Node` is probably already installed on your system, follow [these instructions](https://nodejs.org/en/download/) otherwise.

### Modifying the content

Clone this repository, and checkout the `source-docs` branch

```sh
git clone https://github.com/cltl/cltl.github.io
cd cltl.github.io
git checkout source-docs
```

You can now access and modify the content in `docs` following the [source-docs REAME](https://github.com/cltl/cltl.github.io/blob/source-docs/README.md).

Push your changes to the `source-docs` branch when you are done.

```sh
git push -u origin source-docs
```

### Publishing the site

Go to the `website` folder in the previously cloned `source-docs` branch, and set it up for Docusaurus:

```sh
cd website
yarn install  # this is needed only once after cloning the repository
```

You can view the website locally (on <http://localhost:3000>) by running

```sh
yarn start
```

Generate the website pages by running

```sh
yarn run build
```

If this goes well, you are now ready to deploy the website on <https://cltl.github.io/>. Run

```sh
GIT_USER=<GIT_USER> CURRENT_BRANCH=master yarn run publish-gh-pages
```

where <GIT_USER> is your GitHub username.

If all goes well, the build will be pushed to the `master` branch, with a reference to the current `source-docs` commit. It is therefore important (for a clean git history) that you commit your changes on the `source-docs` branch **before** deploying the website.
